messages.content: hey so I finally finished the literature review for my thesis (thank god) but now I'm kinda stressed about the next phase. my topic is low-resource NLP for under-represented languages which is super interesting but also... overwhelming? like there's so much work to do
That's a really compelling research area! Finishing the lit review is a huge milestone. What's feeling most overwhelming about moving forward?

well Dr. Reeves wants my experiment plan by November which honestly feels super tight? and I keep getting distracted thinking about... other stuff. like I have this ML theory assignment due next week that's eating my brain (sorry, courses bleeding into research thoughts lol)
November does sound tight for a solid experiment plan. Are you thinking of starting with a specific language or language family for your experiments?

yeah I'm leaning toward maybe Quechua or some other indigenous South American languages? but then I started reading about code-switching in multilingual communities and now I'm wondering if I should pivot to that instead... ugh this is why lit reviews are dangerous, you just keep finding cool tangents
Code-switching is fascinating but sounds like it could be a pretty different direction. Have you talked through the scope question with Dr. Reeves? Sometimes advisors can help narrow things down.

we have lab meeting every Friday so I'll probably bring it up then. honestly Reeves has been pretty hands-off which is good but also terrifying? like I want the freedom but also... guidance would be nice sometimes. especially with all the pressure around timelines and stuff

That balance between independence and guidance is tricky in grad school. The Friday lab meetings sound like a good regular touchpoint though. Are you the only one working on low-resource NLP in the lab?

nah there's like two other people doing related stuff but they're PhD students so they're way further along. sometimes I feel like I'm just scrambling to keep up... anyway sorry I'm rambling, I think I just needed to brain dump about this whole experiment planning thing. thanks for listening to my thesis anxiety spiral haha
No worries at all! It sounds like you're making solid progress even if it doesn't always feel like it. Good luck with the experiment planning and that ML theory assignment.

summary: Kai discusses finishing their literature review on low-resource NLP for under-represented languages and feeling overwhelmed about creating an experiment plan by November for Dr. Reeves. The conversation drifts from thesis planning to potential research pivots and lab dynamics.
